questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: You are in the middle of an important dinner party when the waiter spills steaming hot soup all over the hostess. How would you cope?

choices.a: You panic and start shouting on the waiter
choices.b: Arrange for the first aid and make arrangements to rush him or her off to the hospital
choices.c: You dab frantically at the ruined outfit with a napkin while screaming at the waiter
choices.d: You pour the entire contents of a jug of water over his lap, explaining that your prompt action will prevent burns and then lay out a change of clothes in the guest bedroom and return to the party while the boss changes

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: While addressing people in an open ground, it begins to rain. Your address is important and you cannot afford to postpone it. What will you do? [CG PSC 2013]

choices.a: ask someone to arrange an umbrella
choices.b: stop your address for a while
choices.c: continue the address without bothering the rain

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: You are playing football in a park. When you kick the ball, it strikes and breaks the window pane of a nearby house. You would

choices.a: demand your ball back from the house owner
choices.b: say that it was no fault of yours
choices.d: apologise to the house owner and contribute to replace the glass

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: You are an officer incharge for providing basic medical facilities to the survivors of an earthquake affected area. Despite your best possible effort, people put allegations against you for making money out of the funds given for relief. You would [UPSC (CSAT) 2012]

choices.a: let an enquiry be set up to look into the matter
choices.b: ask your senior to appoint some other person in your place
choices.d: stop undertaking any initiative till the matter is resolved

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: You are suffering from diabetes. When you see a whole lot of chocolates, you are tempted to eat them but you also realise that they are not good for you in the long run. What do you do? [Hotel Mgmt 2005]

choices.a: You would not eat them because you know the harmful effects
choices.b: You decide not to eat them but keep thinking about them
choices.c: You would eat them but feel guilty about what you have done
choices.d: You would give in to the temptation and eat the chocolates without being bothered about the consequences

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: You want to get married to a person of your choice, but your family members give their own reasons why you should not marry that person, which you do not find very convincing. What would you do?

choices.a: Go by what your family says
choices.b: Become thoroughly confused and still remain undecided
choices.c: Marry the person of your choice
choices.d: Try to convince your family about your choice

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: You just got to know that your friend has met with an accident and hearing this you immediately leave for the accident spot along with one more friend. In a hurry, you forgot to take your licence and helmet. You were flagged down to stop at the traffic intersection by the traffic constable. You will [CG PSC 2013]

choices.c: stop and reason it out with about the policeman

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: You are handling a priority project and have been meeting all the deadlines and are therefore planning your leave during the project. Your immediate boss does not want your leave citing the urgency of the project. You would [UPSC (CSAT) 2012]

choices.a: proceed on leave without waiting for the sanction
choices.b: pretend to be sick and take leave
choices.c: approach higher authority to reconsider the leave application
choices.d: tell the boss that it is not justified

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: If you find yourself in a situation where you are required to make a power point presentation and you are already bogged down by too much work, as the manager what would you do?

choices.a: Take an alternative mode of presentation
choices.b: Cancel the seminar and reschedule according to your convenience
choices.c: Pass the buck to your subordinate, you are the boss, on one can question you
choices.d: Prioritise your work and try to squeeze out time for it

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: You have worked hard on an idea which you believe would be a breakthrough but the presentation does not go as you had hoped it would. You [Hotel Mgmt 2004]

choices.a: ignore all the suggestions and believe that you were correct
choices.b: break down and get all the emotional
choices.c: take this as a learning experience and convince yourself that you would do better the next time
